key_insight: A pretrained universal ML interatomic potential can already capture key oxygen-plasma/WS2 surface outcomes, and targeted fine-tuning makes it quantitatively accurate for reactive plasma-surface simulation.
engineering_relevance: This suggests plasma-processing models for 2D materials can achieve near-DFT fidelity at much lower computational cost, enabling faster screening and process optimization.
method: Start with the pretrained UMA model (uma-s-1p1) from OC20.
Select diverse WS2 plasma configurations using SOAP descriptors + farthest point sampling.
Fine-tune on DFT labels for energy, forces, and stress at the PBE+D3+U+spin level.
evidence_signal: Without fine-tuning, the model reproduced chemisorbed S and O coverage under 15 eV O+ and O2+ bombardment; after fine-tuning, MAE reached 4.5×10^-3 eV/atom for energy and 0.076 eV/Å for forces.
watch_item: The pretrained model matched key observables even without spin polarization and Hubbard U, so deployment should verify whether these physics omissions remain acceptable for the target plasma condition.
paper_title: Fine-Tuning a Universal Machine-Learned Interatomic Potential for Oxygen Plasma Interactions with WS$_2$
